description: Smaller natural watercourses take the name 'phuliro'. Some streams run year-round; others return with rain or snowmelt. 'luphore' (river) names the larger side of the local distinction rather than a size fixed for every landscape.
articulatory_notes: For unvoiced bilabial 'ph', bring the lips close and let the narrow breath enter high-back rounded 'u'. Touch the tongue tip to the alveolar ridge for lateral 'l' and release voice around its sides into stressed high-front 'i'. Tap or briefly trill the tongue at the ridge, then finish with middle-height rounded 'o'.

semantic_domains.nature: a small natural watercourse within a landscape
semantic_domains.spatial: a narrow channel that may feed or leave another body of water
